engine_overheating.solution: Stop engine immediately. Let cool. Check coolant level. Clean radiator fins. Inspect fan belt tension. Replace thermostat if faulty.

engine_not_starting.solution: Check battery charge and terminals. Check fuel level and lines. Clean or replace air filter. Bleed fuel system.

causes.black: excess fuel or restricted air intake
causes.white: water in fuel or head gasket failure
causes.blue: engine oil burning due to worn rings or seals

excessive_smoke.solution: Black smoke: clean air filter, check fuel injection. White smoke: check head gasket, drain fuel if water contaminated. Blue smoke: engine overhaul needed.

hydraulic_failure.solution: Check hydraulic oil level. Replace hydraulic filter. Inspect pump for wear. Check for oil leaks at connections.

steering_problem.solution: Check and inflate tyres equally. Top up power steering fluid. Inspect steering linkage for wear.

low_discharge.solution: Check water table depth. Clean strainer. Prime pump to remove air lock. If impeller worn, call technician.

symptoms: motor hums but does not start
pump_not_starting.solution: Check voltage supply. Replace start capacitor. If winding burned, motor rewinding needed.

excessive_sand.solution: Check and repair well screen. Lower pump setting. Install sand separator.

grain_damage.solution: Reduce cylinder speed. Increase concave clearance. Allow crop to dry before threshing.

poor_grain_separation.solution: Clean sieves regularly. Adjust fan speed. Reduce feed rate.

spray_machine.calibration: Calibrate sprayer before each season. A worn nozzle can deliver 20 to 30 percent more than rated output, leading to overdose and crop damage.
